preamble: The transformation of the voiced labiodental fricative /v/ into the voiced postalveolar fricative /ʒ/ is a fascinating example of palatalization, a sound change where a consonant's articulation moves towards the palate due to the influence of an adjacent palatal sound, often a high front vowel or a /j/ glide. This shift typically involves a change in the place of articulation from the front of the mouth (labiodental) to a more central, postalveolar position, sometimes passing through an intermediate affricate stage.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, place assimilation (labiodental to postalveolar), affrication (intermediate), deaffrication

examples.note: Latin /v/ followed by /j/ (from -ia) palatalized to an affricate, then deaffricated to /ʒ/.
Latin /v/ followed by /j/ palatalized to an affricate, then deaffricated to /ʒ/.

sources: How did the Latin /p/, /b/, /v/ sometimes become /ʒ/ in French? : r/linguistics - Reddit. (July 30, 2022).
Palatalization in the Romance languages - Wikipedia.
